commit.message:
Preserve original WAV format on save

Save and export now write back in the original format (PCM 16/24-bit
or IEEE float 32-bit, mono or stereo) instead of always converting to
stereo 16-bit PCM.

repository.name: move-anything-waveform-editor
repository.description: Waveform Editor tool for Move Everything - trim, gain adjust, normalize, and edit WAV files on the Ableton Move

commit.message:
Refactor DSP to stereo, fix drum preset export, and misc improvements

- Refactor DSP from mono int16 to interleaved stereo int16 (L,R,L,R,...)
- Mono WAV files duplicated to both channels on load
- Support PCM 16-bit, PCM 24-bit, and IEEE float 32-bit input
- WAV output always stereo 16-bit PCM
- Waveform display and zero-crossing use L channel
- Fix DSP export symbol: plugin_init -> move_plugin_init_v2
- Fix build.sh to copy from src/dsp/dsp.so (Docker output path)
- Fix drum preset sampleUri: URL-encode spaces (%20)
- Default auto slice threshold from 5 to 25
- Disable loop playback in slice mode, restore on exit
- Back from record-ready state exits module entirely

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: move-anything-waveform-editor
repository.description: Waveform Editor tool for Move Everything - trim, gain adjust, normalize, and edit WAV files on the Ableton Move

commit.message:
Fix recording race condition and improve recorded file handling

- Add "stopping" state to poll sampler finalization before loading WAV,
  preventing "No waveform data" error from async stop
- Show "SAVING..." screen while waiting for WAV to finalize
- Track recorded files as unsaved (dirty) with discard-on-exit cleanup
- Add timestamp to edit filenames (sample_edit_0306_1423.wav)
- Clean up recording UI header (remove debug VU display)
- Add dirty param support to DSP plugin

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: move-anything-waveform-editor
repository.description: Waveform Editor tool for Move Everything - trim, gain adjust, normalize, and edit WAV files on the Ableton Move

commit.message:
Remap knobs: E4=vertical scale, E5=gain/threshold, E7/E8=twist actions

- E3: zoom only (removed shift+E3 vertical scale)
- E4: vertical scale for all modes (was shift+E3)
- E5: gain/normalize in trim/loop, threshold in slice auto (was E4)
- E7 touch+twist: toggle loop on/off
- E8 touch+twist: snap to zero crossing
- Twist prompts stay visible while knob is touched
- Add snapToZeroCrossing() helper function
- Recording waveform: scrolling write-head visualization
- Recording stop: poll for sampler finalization before loading
- Shift+pad preview: 5% of selection, clamped 0.25s-5s
- Update help.json for new knob layout

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: move-anything-waveform-editor
repository.description: Waveform Editor tool for Move Everything - trim, gain adjust, normalize, and edit WAV files on the Ableton Move

commit.message:
Fix false positives in exploit/scan modules (FW3.7.1)

- Add _discover_params(): crawl real page params before testing
- SQLi: use baseline comparison to filter pre-existing SQL-like text
- SQLi: time-based threshold now requires +4s vs baseline (not raw 2.5s)
- XSS: use unique session marker instead of generic payloads
- XSS: remove DOM sink false positives (innerHTML/eval exist everywhere)
- CMDi: use echo CMDI_CONFIRMED marker, remove generic 'ttl' indicator
- LFI: remove empty indicator payload (matched everything)
- LFI: use specific indicators (root:x:0:0:, HTTP_HOST=)
- vBulletin: require 'vbulletin' in response body (not just 302)
- upload_check: only report 200 with upload form content (not 403)
- sqli_scan: discover real params from page before injecting

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: webgate
repository.description: webgate fw by c3less

commit.message:
Release WebGate FW3.7

New features:
- SSH backdoor via RCE CVE with Scope Agreement (typed 'I accept')
- TOR routing via bridge (obfs4/webtunnel/snowflake)
- SQLMap interactive dump dialog: pick DB -> table -> dump in GUI
- CVEExploitOfferDialog: auto-prompt when RCE CVE detected after scan
- ScopeAgreementDialog: typed 'I accept' confirmation before exploit
- SSHBackdoorDialog: configure SSH credentials + TOR bridge
- SQLMapDumpDialog: GUI-based DB/table selector with background dump

Improvements:
- Simplified reports: SQL injection Y/N + vulnerable param/payload
- XSStrike output simplified to injection Y/N + vulnerable URL
- Progress bar with gradient, % overlay, bright pulse edge animation
- Logo glitch animation (random interval glitch effect)
- Particle burst animation on scan start
- FW badge in logo canvas
- Scan-line border animation during active scan

Fixes:
- Removed COPY button (caused GUI freeze/lag on Wayland)
- Version strings updated to FW3.7
- LICENSE added (authorized use only)
- README.md fully rewritten with all features documented

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: UI overhaul - compact modals, email verification polling, backup codes popup

- Compact modal system: reduced padding, thin scrollbars, dvh mobile support
- Email verification: 5s polling with in-memory dedup (inspired by reg-bot pattern)
- MIME parsing fallback: concatenate parsed body + raw for code extraction

- 2FA layout: time offset moved to same row as algorithm/digits
- Edit modal: email row emoji buttons (✏️ name, 📝 notes with notepad style)

- 5 ATK-scaling + 5 INT-scaling, ATK range 340-400 (surpass all existing)

  - Kusanagi: crit stacking ATK + x5 at 10 stacks + permanent DEF ignore
  - Gae Bolg: first attack x3 + periodic dash AoE every 10s
  - Masamune: conditional DMG bonus + kill heal + 2 lethal absorbs
  - Longinus: permanent DEF ignore + boss bonus + crit sacred wave
  - Tyrfing: kill stacking ATK/lifesteal + low HP rage + invincible
  - Ea Staff: 30% free cast + high mana DMG bonus
  - Fragarach: hit count tornado AoE
  - Tacos Eternel: confusion debuff + infinite kill stacking + kebab AoE
  - Ame-no-nuhoko: +50% heals + ally resurrection + team stat buff

commit.message:
Fix bugs found in audit: thumbnail generator, mobile sign-out, UI overflow

- Fix thumbnail AI image generator: image was hidden from DOM while loading
  so onLoad never fired, leaving the spinner stuck forever. Now mounts the
  img immediately (display:none) so onLoad/onError can fire correctly.
- Add sign-out button to Settings page, accessible on mobile where the
  desktop sidebar user section is not shown.
- Hide ideas vault title pills on mobile to prevent overflow.
- Abbreviate day labels (Sun→S) in both calendars on mobile to fit 7 columns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: simplify external module storage and add module management commands (#47)

- Change external module storage from cache to user config directory
- Makes modules easily editable without cache invalidation issues
- Add priority-based resolution: profile -> external -> system

- `fedpunk module available` - Lists modules available to install
- `fedpunk module update <name|all>` - Pulls git updates and redeploys if changed

Clean script for building and installing Fedpunk from a git checkout:
- Validates git state and warns about uncommitted changes
- Checks for build dependencies
- Creates proper source tarball from HEAD
- Builds RPM with patched spec file
- Installs with dnf

Usage:
  ./install-from-source.sh           # Build and install
  ./install-from-source.sh --build   # Build only
  ./install-from-source.sh --clean   # Clean artifacts

* feat(sources): add multi-module source repository support

Add sources concept for managing collections of external modules:
- Create sources.fish library for clone/update/discover operations
- Add fedpunk source CLI (add, list, sync, modules, remove)
- Update module resolver with priority: profile → sources → external → native
- Sync sources automatically before deployment
- Update module installed/available to show source modules

Sources are multi-module git repos stored in ~/.config/fedpunk/sources/
Direct git URLs are stored in ~/.config/fedpunk/modules/

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

* feat(sources): add registry format support for source repos

Sources can now be registry repos with modules.yaml that map
module names to their git URLs. This allows a single source
to reference modules in separate git repositories.

Registry format:
```yaml
modules:
  module-name:
    repo: git@gitlab.com:org/module.git
    description: Module description
```

When deploying from a registry, modules are cloned to
~/.config/fedpunk/modules/ like direct git URLs.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- fedpunk module source remove <url>

This makes the CLI hierarchy more intuitive since sources

* feat(ssh): check stable socket before starting new agent

Checks for ~/.ssh/agent.sock (stable symlink from systemd ssh-agent)
before spawning a new agent. This enables reliable SSH agent forwarding
to containers that mount the stable socket path.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ssh): use correct exit codes for ssh-add agent detection

ssh-add -l returns:
- 0: keys listed (connected)
- 1: no identities (connected but empty)
- 2: cannot connect to agent

Previously checked for exit 0, which failed when agent was running
but had no keys loaded. Now correctly checks for exit != 2.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(ssh): add fish config for stable SSH agent socket

Sets SSH_AUTH_SOCK to ~/.ssh/agent.sock on shell startup if the stable
socket exists. This ensures containers can reliably mount the socket
and SSH agent forwarding works consistently.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

When fedpunk ssh load starts a new agent, create a symlink at
~/.ssh/agent.sock pointing to the actual socket. This ensures

The fish config now checks if the stable socket actually works
(agent responds) before setting SSH_AUTH_SOCK, instead of just
checking if the socket file exists.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(spec): prevent .config directory from being owned by root

The %post script was creating ~/.config/fedpunk with mkdir -p, which
also created the parent .config directory as root. The chown only fixed
the fedpunk subdirectory, leaving .config owned by root.

Now tracks if .config was created and fixes its ownership too.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(profile): preserve git URLs in fedpunk.yaml for declarative config (#49)

* feat(profile): preserve git URLs in fedpunk.yaml for declarative config

Enable users to declaratively reference git profiles in fedpunk.yaml
by URL, similar to how sources work. The system automatically clones
and updates profiles from git URLs.

Changes:
- Remove line that overwrote profile_to_save with extracted repo name
- Add logic to save basename only for path-based profiles
- Git URLs are now preserved in config instead of being extracted to name
- Add comprehensive test script for git profile URL functionality

Example usage:
```yaml
# Git URL (auto-clones/updates on apply)
profile: git@github.com:user/hyprpunk.git
mode: laptop

# Name (resolves from profile directories)
profile: default
mode: desktop
```

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(module): add declarative environment variable support

Modules can now declare environment variables in module.yaml under an
`environment:` section. Users can also declare global environment
variables in fedpunk.yaml.

Features:
- New env-injector.fish generates ~/.config/fish/conf.d/fedpunk-module-env.fish
- Supports $FEDPUNK_PARAM_* interpolation for dynamic values
- User-defined env vars in fedpunk.yaml override module defaults
- Env config is sourced immediately after deployment

Example module.yaml:
  environment:
    CLAUDE_CODE_USE_VERTEX: "1"
    CLOUD_ML_REGION: "$FEDPUNK_PARAM_CLAUDE_REGION"

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(config): use clean environment for yq to avoid shell pollution (#50)

Fedora container images have kiwi_* environment variables that echo
to stdout during shell initialization, polluting yq output.

Added _yq_safe wrapper that runs yq with 'env -i' to avoid picking
up garbage from the shell environment.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(yq): apply clean environment wrapper across all yq calls

The previous fix in config.fish only addressed yq calls in that file.
This comprehensive fix:

- Creates shared lib/fish/yq-utils.fish with _yq_safe and _yq_safe_eval
- Updates all files that use yq to source the shared utilities
- Replaces all yq read operations with _yq_safe/_yq_safe_eval
- Prevents kiwi_* environment variable pollution in Fedora containers

Files updated:
- config.fish: Sources shared yq-utils.fish
- deployer.fish: Uses _yq_safe for module list
- env-injector.fish: Uses _yq_safe for environment reading
- module-ref-parser.fish: Uses _yq_safe_eval for parsing
- param-injector.fish: Uses _yq_safe_eval for parameter extraction
- param-prompter.fish: Uses _yq_safe_eval for config reading
- sources.fish: Uses _yq_safe for registry operations
- yaml-parser.fish: Uses _yq_safe/_yq_safe_eval for parsing

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(env): write environment configs to system-wide locations

Generate /etc/fish/conf.d/fedpunk-env.fish and /etc/profile.d/fedpunk-env.sh

* fix(env): write environment configs to user-level locations

Previously env-injector wrote to /etc/fish/conf.d/ and /etc/profile.d/
which required root access and failed silently without sudo.

Now writes to user-level config:
- Fish: ~/.config/fish/conf.d/fedpunk-module-env.fish (auto-sourced)
- Bash: ~/.config/fedpunk/profile.d/fedpunk-env.sh (source from .bashrc)

This matches how param-injector works and fixes the file path expected
by fedpunk-module.fish line 464.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(deploy): generate env config after module deployment

Previously, env-generate-fish-config was called BEFORE modules were
deployed, causing module environment variables to not be included
if the modules weren't yet in place (e.g., first-time deployment).

Now env and param config generation happens AFTER all modules are
deployed, ensuring all module.yaml files are available for parsing.

This fixes the issue where environment variables from user modules
(like CLAUDE_CODE_USE_VERTEX) weren't being generated during
fedpunk apply.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(config): prevent duplicate modules in fedpunk.yaml

Use fedpunk-config-list-enabled-modules for the duplicate check,
which properly extracts module names from both simple string and
object formats (module: name with params).

Previously the check used raw YAML which didn't match object entries.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(module-resolver): source paths.fish for FEDPUNK_USER/FEDPUNK_SYSTEM

The module resolver uses FEDPUNK_USER to find profile modules and
FEDPUNK_SYSTEM to find system modules, but these environment
variables weren't guaranteed to be set when the library was sourced
directly (e.g., during env-generate-fish-config).

This caused profile modules like 'claude' to not be found during
environment variable generation, even though they exist in the
profile's modules directory.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(config): prevent duplicate modules from profile's mode.yaml

Extend duplicate detection in fedpunk-config-add-module to also check
the active profile's mode.yaml modules list. Previously, running
`fedpunk module deploy <name>` would add a module to modules.enabled
even if it was already part of the deployed profile.

Changes:
- Add fedpunk-config-list-profile-modules helper function
- Check profile modules before adding to modules.enabled
- Add null check and -- flag to contains for robustness

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Migrate todos and streak from JSON files to SQLite via Prisma

schema.prisma:
- Todo model (id, timestamp, text, priority, due_date)
- Streak singleton model (id=1, current_streak, last_completed_date, longest_streak)

db.py (new shared data-access layer):
- get_todos() — fetches all rows, returns dicts sorted by urgency
- create_todo() / delete_todo()
- get_streak() / update_streak() — upserts the singleton row
- migrate_from_json_if_needed() — one-time import of todos.json + streak.json

server.py:
- /api/todos and /api/streak now delegate to db.*
- Removed duplicate urgency_key sort logic (now lives only in db.py)

countdown.py:
- All todo/streak I/O replaced with db.* calls
- self.todos reloaded from db after every mutation (always pre-sorted)
- delete_item and mark_done use direct index into pre-sorted self.todos
- Removed _urgency_key method (moved to db.py)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add SPA rewrite rule for react-router client-side routing

Without this, direct navigation to /explore, /activity, or /settings

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Anchor comments to text content and fix real-time sidebar updates (#2)

- Store quoted text alongside charStart/charEnd to anchor comments to actual text
- Implement ProseMirror transaction mapping to track positions in real-time as doc changes
- Add re-anchoring logic for cross-session persistence using text search
- Share comment state between EditorPageClient and CommentThread to fix sidebar updates
- Add error handling for stale user sessions (P2003 foreign key violations)
- Improve JWT validation to detect missing users on refresh

This ensures comments stay with the text they were placed on, even when surrounding content is edited, and appear immediately in the sidebar regardless of order.

commit.message:
feat(mobile): add proagent-mobile plugin (#35)

## Summary

- Adds `proagent-mobile`, a new practice plugin for mobile app
development with Expo and React Native
- Wraps 10 MIT-licensed skills from
[expo/skills](https://github.com/expo/skills) (© 650 Industries, Inc.)
following the marketplace plugin pattern
- Wires the plugin into `proagent-router` so `/proagent <mobile
request>` routes automatically

## What's included

**New plugin** (`plugins/proagent-mobile/`):
- 1 registered skill (`mobile-assistant`) — thin router, <80 lines, Ask
First/Load Later pattern
- 34 reference files (10 Expo skill files + 24 sub-references), loaded
lazily at point-of-need
- 3 commands: `proagent-mobile-hub`, `proagent-mobile-run` (7 modes),
`proagent-mobile-review` (3 modes)
- 1 specialist agent (`mobile-specialist`, sonnet)
- 3 lifecycle hooks: EAS build guard, app config watcher, session
completion reminder
- `LICENSE` file with Expo attribution

**Skills covered:** `building-native-ui`, `expo-dev-client`,
`expo-deployment`, `native-data-fetching`, `expo-api-routes`,
`expo-cicd-workflows`, `expo-tailwind-setup`, `upgrading-expo`,
`expo-android-safe-area`, `use-dom`

**Updated files:**
- `marketplace.json` — added entry, stats updated (18 → 19 plugins, 867
→ 901 assets)
- `.claude-plugin/marketplace.json` — added entry
- `README.md` — added row to plugins table, updated statistics
- `proagent-router` — SKILL.md routing table, proagent.md practice list,
CLAUDE.md coverage list

## License

Skills are MIT licensed from `expo/skills`. Attribution is included in
`plugins/proagent-mobile/LICENSE` and `README.md`.

## Test plan

- [ ] Verify `/proagent-mobile-hub` lists all 10 capabilities
- [ ] Verify `/proagent-mobile-run build` loads `expo-dev-client.md`
reference
- [ ] Verify `/proagent-mobile-run deploy` loads `expo-deployment.md`
and sub-references
- [ ] Verify `/proagent-mobile-review app-config` runs the config audit
- [ ] Verify `/proagent set up EAS Build` routes to `mobile-specialist`
- [ ] Confirm SKILL.md is under 80 lines and contains no bash code
blocks

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Simplify version specs to only * and == (#78)

## Summary

- Remove `^` and `>=` version specifiers from docs and tests, keeping
only `*` (latest) and `==` (exact match)
- Update all doc examples: `^1.0.0` → `*`, `>=1.0.0` → `==1.0.0`
- Remove `^` and `>=` rows from version specifier reference table
- Update `test_delegation.py` to use `==` instead of `^`

Companion to strawpot/strawhub#95 which implements the same
simplification on the registry side.

## Test plan

- [x] `test_delegation.py` updated and passes
- [ ] Verify docs render correctly

commit.message:
Fix Docker build: install curl in server image, remove db host port, fix status filter

- server/Dockerfile: install curl for healthcheck (not in aspnet base image)
- docker-compose.yml: remove db port 5432 (conflicts with host postgres)
- DevicesController: move status filter into EF query to fix CS1061 build error
  (anonymous object returned as 'object' was inaccessible for LINQ filtering)

All three containers verified running: db (healthy), server (JWT login works),
frontend (nginx serving SPA on port 80).

Remove recent vibes strip from metrics section

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Phase 4 — WebSocket real-time sync

- ws/hub.go: HubManager (board-scoped hub lifecycle) + Hub (register/unregister/broadcast select loop)
- ws/client.go: ReadPump/WritePump with ping/pong (30s interval, 40s timeout)
- ws/message.go: WSMessage type with Type, Payload, UserID
- handler/ws.go: GET /api/v1/ws?board_id= with manual JWT verification + gorilla/websocket upgrade
- handler/task.go: broadcast task:created/updated/deleted/moved after successful DB ops
- handler/column.go: broadcast column:created/updated/deleted/reordered after successful DB ops
- Added GetColumnByID query + BoardService.GetBoardIDByColumnID for board_id resolution
- member:joined/member:left events on WebSocket connect/disconnect
- Hub auto-cleanup when last client disconnects

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: resolve HOME path mismatch, fontconfig, and SDK version in CI

- Install fontconfig in container
- Use $HOME instead of hardcoded /root (GitHub sets HOME=/github/home)
- Auto-detect and copy export templates to correct location
- Auto-detect Java SDK and Android SDK paths
- Set target_sdk=33 to match available build-tools
- Remove hardcoded keystore path from preset (use editor settings)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add work path change detection for mod list refresh

When profile work path configuration changes, automatically detect the
change and refresh mod list and category tree only if the path actually
changed. This prevents unnecessary refreshes while ensuring the UI updates
when the cache directory location changes.

Changes:
- Add workPathRef to track current work path in ModProvider
- Create checkWorkPathChange() function to compare and detect changes
- Subscribe to PROFILE.CONFIG_UPDATED event
- Only refresh mods/categories when work path actually changes
- Replace console with logger for proper logging

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Enhance sidebar navigation with new links for better user experience.

platform-specific notes. All agents (Know-it-All, Precon, Design

commit.message:
feat: group stories into briefing batches with navigation (#8)

Stories are now presented one briefing (digest) at a time instead of
flattened across all digests. After completing a briefing, users see an
interstitial with options to continue to the next briefing or mark all
as read. A "Finish Briefing" shortcut in the story footer allows
skipping remaining stories in the current briefing.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: show lead temperature in instructor dashboard table

Adds Temp (🔥/🌡️/❄️ signal) and Score (0–100) columns to the leads
table, computed per row via compute_lead_temperature with None for
quiz/reflection inputs, consistent with the detail-panel integration.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add Lazy SMP multi-threaded search

Spawn N threads each running iterative_deepening independently,
sharing the transposition table and pawn hash via Arc. Thread 0
is authoritative (outputs UCI info/bestmove), helper threads only
populate the TT at offset start depths for tree diversity. Node
counts are synced via Arc<AtomicU64> for accurate NPS reporting.
Defaults to Threads=1 with zero overhead.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Limit address verification to once per 30 days per restaurant

Skip restaurants whose lastGeocodedAt is within the last 30 days.
Previously all restaurants were cycled through as fast as the daily
reserve allowed (~10 days with 411 restaurants at 40/day).

commit.message:
Make authentication mandatory on all pages

Previously auth only ran on app.py — users could bypass it by navigating
directly to any page URL. Now every page calls require_auth() which shows
the login form and calls st.stop() if not authenticated.

Extracted shared auth logic into src/utils/auth.py to avoid duplication.

commit.message:
fix: trigger clean Vercel rebuild to restore CSS

Previous CLI deployments via `vercel deploy` deployed unprocessed
CSS with raw @tailwind directives. This empty commit triggers a
proper git-based Vercel build with PostCSS/Tailwind processing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix: remove VITE_API_URL/VITE_WS_URL from netlify.toml build.environment

netlify.toml [build.environment] values override Netlify dashboard env vars,
so the empty defaults were silently overriding the Railway URLs set in the
dashboard. Removing them lets the dashboard values take effect.
